title: Headache

red_flags.text: Defines adult headache telehealth scope in DHA-licensed facilities and emergency exclusions.
red_flags.rationale: Supports adult telehealth headache presentation context without extending to children or emergency treatment.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines adult headache telehealth scope in DHA-licensed facilities and emergency exclusions.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Headaches in Adults

red_flags.text: Provides systemic, neurologic, onset, associated-feature, and pattern-change red-flag domains.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped seizure, neck-stiffness, fever, and visual red-flag or assessed-negative fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Provides systemic, neurologic, onset, associated-feature, and pattern-change red-flag domains.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Headaches in Adults

red_flags.text: Identifies headache presentations requiring urgent face-to-face assessment.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-decided imaging, safety, and urgent-evaluation documentation.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Identifies headache presentations requiring urgent face-to-face assessment.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Headaches in Adults

red_flags.text: Supports sudden onset, neurological deficit, fever, trauma, pattern change, unexplained vomiting, further investigation or referral, and structured headache-diary fields.

red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports sudden onset, neurological deficit, fever, trauma, pattern change, unexplained vomiting, further investigation or referral, and structured headache-diary fields.

red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports recording headache frequency, duration, severity, intervention effectiveness, impact, and clinician-led further investigation or referral when red flags develop.

source_limitations: Children younger than 12 years and nontelehealth emergency pathways are outside the exact source set.

The “neurological headache” alias, detailed examination fields, blood pressure or pulse review, CBC, CRP, prior imaging or neurology-note review, medication plans, generic clinician plans, and fixed follow-up remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, imaging interpretation, treatment, and emergency disposition remain clinician-stated; qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
